frames.name: Media Hypocrisy Frame
frames.description: Presents the story as evidence of double standards in sports media coverage, contrasting criticism of athletes attending Trump events with past support for athletes attending Obama events
frames.emphasis: Historical inconsistency in media treatment of athletes' White House visits based on the president's political party

frames.name: Political Exploitation Frame
frames.description: Focuses on criticism that the hockey team allowed themselves to be used as political props by the Trump administration
frames.emphasis: The appropriateness of using Olympic athletes for political purposes and the athletes' agency in participating

frames.name: Bipartisan Unity Frame
frames.description: Highlights the hockey team's appearance as one of the few moments that received positive bipartisan support during the State of the Union
frames.emphasis: The rare moment of cross-party agreement and positive polling among independent voters

outlierPerspectives: Breitbart's characterization of Peter Rosenberg's comments as 'racist remarks' for referencing the players' 'white, cushy, privileged' lives
The specific comparison to Tim Thomas's 2012 decision to skip the Obama White House visit as a contrasting example of media treatment
missingContext: The full context and transcript of Peter Rosenberg's original comments
Responses or statements from the hockey team members themselves about their participation
Historical precedent for Olympic teams attending State of the Union addresses
The specific circumstances of the team's invitation and decision-making process
Broader context about athlete activism and political participation in recent years
